signals.headline: XRP Gains Full Commodity Classification from SEC and CFTC | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Gains Full Commodity Classification from SEC and CFTC | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Confirmed recently (SEC on March 17, 2026), removing long-standing uncertainty; spurred $1.21B in XRP ETF inflows, positioning it for pension/sovereign wealth entry if CLARITY Act passes.[4][3][6] Enhances institutional appeal in cross-border payments via RippleNet/ODL.[3][5]

signals.headline: HEADLINE: Ohio Lt
signals.raw: HEADLINE: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted, appointed to fill JD Vance's Senate seat after Vance's VP resignation, announces 2026 midterm run to complete Vance's term against Democratic challenger ex-Sen. Sherrod Brown[3].**
matched_markets: Will the Democratic Party control the Senate after the 2026 Midterm elections?

signals.headline: HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
HIGH confidence**

signals.headline: Oil prices have driven the inflationary surge, with oil products jumping 9
signals.raw: Oil prices have driven the inflationary surge, with oil products jumping 9.9% in March—the fastest increase in almost three and a half years—contributing 0.39 percentage points to overall inflation[2]

signals.headline: Governor Rhee Chang-yong stated that this year's consumer price inflation is now expected to "significantly exceed" the
signals.raw: Governor Rhee Chang-yong stated that this year's consumer price inflation is now expected to "significantly exceed" the initial 2.2% forecast from February, with the core inflation rate also anticipated to be higher than the forecasted 2.1%[1]

signals.headline: The central bank projects inflation to reach the mid to upper two-percent range for 2026[4]
signals.raw: The central bank projects inflation to reach the mid to upper two-percent range for 2026[4]

signals.headline: The Korean won has weakened significantly to around 1,480-1,520 won per U
signals.raw:
The Korean won has weakened significantly to around 1,480-1,520 won per U.S. dollar, driven by Middle East tensions and foreign investor stock sales[1][4]

The decision maintains the status quo while policymakers monitor the Middle East conflict as the primary external risk factor affecting monetary policy[1].
